rationale: Lens: impacted-domains parity + decomposition, re-verified in-tree at eb6a2ed1. The item-8 ownership move is now REAL, not merely claimed: workflow/OWNERSHIP.yaml gained `+internal/lifecycle/**` and execution/OWNERSHIP.yaml lost it (git log: round-1 draft a0a06943 added it to execution; revision removed+relocated). Exactly ONE domain (workflow) claims lifecycle — grep confirms 0 hits in execution — so AC10's execution-negative discriminator genuinely fires (FALSE at round-1 SHA, TRUE now). Parity is clean: every touched path maps to its declared owner — approve/complete/lifecycle/setup/.claude+.agents skills all workflow-owned; executor comment-only + fixtures execution-owned. Epic-scoped detection (item 4) verified in orphans.go:74-112 (findEpicBySpecIDFn → listClosedBeadsFn(epicID)), so a different-spec orphan can neither trigger nor silence the refusal; blp6 stays a disclosed P2. Load-bearing groundings all hold: claude.go:681 lifecycleSkillFiles / :736 ms-impl-approve, both materialized copies exist, impl.go:140 EnsureMigrated, :226-227 planErr skip, :452 readPlanBeadIDs, FinalizeEpic loop, 'rule as CompleteBead' ×1. Decomposition is a coherent ~2-3 beads (gate+lifecycle-refactor+complete-extract; tests; docs/ADR/skill/ownership); the error-preserving refactor is a mechanical no-behavior-change extract-and-wrap and does not over-fill bead 1. Lifecycle code byte-unchanged vs main.

findings.detail: R1 specs an error-preserving core (ScanOrphanedClosedBeads) + fail-open wrapper parity for complete/next/doctor; OQ2 rewritten to advisory-fail-open / gate-fail-closed asymmetry; AC1(b) pins the infra-error refusal AND unchanged wrapper behavior in internal/lifecycle tests.
R3 refuses naming the plan path on unreadable/corrupt plan bead IDs instead of inheriting impl.go:226-227's silent skip; AC6(e) covers corrupt plan.md.

R1 narrows the guarantee to 'no close/phase-write/merge/push' and explicitly carves out phase.EnsureMigrated at impl.go:140 (verified: that line is the one-shot migration reconcile preceding every gate).

Verified in code: orphans.go:74-112 scopes enumeration to the finalizing spec's epic. R1 + AC2(c) prove a different-spec orphan does not refuse; blp6 merge-loop fix explicitly DEFERRED P2 (Background + Out of Scope).

Verified: claude.go:681 lifecycleSkillFiles(), :736 ms-impl-approve entry, and both .claude/skills + .agents/skills materialized copies exist. AC9 uses the refusal-specific 'closed without' (discriminating) not bare 'mindspec complete'.
Verified: 'rule as CompleteBead' occurs exactly once in internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go on a single line — the removed-line and post-change greps are matchable.

AC10's execution-file negative discriminator is genuinely FALSE at the round-1 SHA (a0a06943 had lifecycle in execution) and TRUE now — verified via git log. AC7 names TestRecoveryConvergence_AlreadyClosedOrphan with an existence check that matches zero tests today.

VERIFIED IN-TREE: workflow/OWNERSHIP.yaml has internal/lifecycle/**; execution/OWNERSHIP.yaml does not (grep 0). Exactly one claimant. Impacted Domains + AC10 both point at workflow. Lifecycle code byte-unchanged vs main (diff empty) — a pure ownership relocation.
Goal discloses both residuals (raw-git-merge'd never-refuted-RC bead leaving no obligation; cross-spec blp6 orphan) and R3's coverage is qualified 'only when a durable obligation was recorded'.
OQ2 rationale reframed to the asymmetry (no Dolt-hiccup framing); orphans.go bound corrected to :74-112; Background parity sentence tightened.

Background frames 115 as the impl-approve leg of 4gsz's detect-and-block floor via the shared FindOrphanedClosedBeads predicate; complete/next/doctor legs shipped under 4gsz; the CI leg stays 4gsz scope (Out of Scope). No other 4gsz leg pulled into 115.

LOW / non-blocking. Impacted Domains lists cmd/** in the workflow bullet, but no cmd/ file appears in Scope's In-Scope list — a minor path-granularity over-declaration. The workflow DOMAIN is genuinely impacted (approve/complete/lifecycle/setup/skills), and doc-sync operates at domain granularity, so this does not affect correctness or the gate. Optional tidy: drop cmd/** from the enumeration or note it as illustrative. Not a REQUEST_CHANGES.

LOW / pre-existing, out of 115 scope (carried from round 1). execution/OWNERSHIP.yaml is edited by the spec branch (removing the interim lifecycle add) yet is not itself claimed by any domain, unlike workflow/core which self-own their OWNERSHIP.yaml. mindspec-validate is claimed passing (advisory WARN acceptable). Optional consistency improvement, not a blocker.
